qualitative_claim: An investment may only proceed to analysis if the analyst can explain in plain language how the business makes money, who its customers are, and why those customers will continue to buy
qualitative_rule: Reject any investment where the analyst cannot articulate — without jargon, technology trend extrapolation, or industry-insider assumptions — the business model, the source of customer demand, and a plausible long-run trajectory
anti_scope: Does not prohibit investment in complex industries; prohibits investment in any business where the analyst's understanding is shallow or borrowed rather than genuine
rationale: The practitioner's knowledge boundary is self-defined and defended with unusual rigidity — passing on entire sectors including technology and biotech under heavy social pressure. Genuine understanding is operationally defined as the ability to explain the business simply and predict its state a decade out with real confidence.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 comparisons confirm this seed covers a distinct rule_subject not replicated across Duan or Munger seeds. The plain-language articulation test — covering business model, customer demand source, and long-run trajectory — is an operationally specific formulation that avoids vague 'understand the business' platitudes. Anti_scope correctly distinguishes shallow versus genuine understanding. No direction-of-change challenge surfaced.

qualitative_claim: A target business must possess an identifiable structural competitive barrier that protects its returns on capital from competitive erosion
qualitative_rule: Reject businesses where no identifiable source of durable competitive advantage — brand power, cost leadership, network effects, switching costs, or regulatory barriers — can be articulated and supported by observable business economics
anti_scope: Does not require the structural competitive barrier to be impenetrable or permanent; requires only that a meaningful structural advantage exists and can be identified with specificity
rationale: Structural competitive barrier analysis is the single most important qualitative factor in this methodology's mature framework. Without a structural competitive advantage, high returns on capital will be competed away over time, destroying the foundation for long-term compounding.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 confirms no equivalent seed in either comparative corpus. Structural competitive barrier existence as a hard veto is foundational to this methodology's mature framework. The five named sources (brand, cost leadership, network effects, switching costs, regulatory barriers) provide sufficient operational specificity without over-constraining the evaluator. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: A structural competitive barrier that is demonstrably narrowing on a secular basis is a disqualifying condition, even if the barrier currently appears wide
qualitative_rule: If evidence across a minimum of three consecutive full fiscal years shows the competitive position has been systematically losing ground — through declining market share, pricing power erosion, or successful competitive encroachment — reject the investment regardless of the apparent current competitive barrier strength; a single year of deterioration does not satisfy this criterion
anti_scope: Does not apply to temporary or cyclical competitive pressure; does not apply to deterioration observed over fewer than three full fiscal years; applies only to structural, secular erosion supported by a credible mechanism of disruption and confirmed across the minimum observation window
rationale: A prominent practitioner's largest analytical failures — newspapers and IBM — involved structural competitive barriers that were real at purchase but eroded faster than expected. The critical diagnostic is not whether the structural competitive barrier exists today but whether it is widening or narrowing. Adding a three-year minimum observation window prevents false-positive vetoes from cyclical noise while preserving the protective intent of the rule.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 found no equivalent seed, confirming uniqueness. However, internal review reveals that the qualitative_rule lacks a temporal anchor for 'multiple years' of erosion evidence — without one, the rule can be gamed by treating any three-quarter slippage as 'confirmed secular decline' or, conversely, dismissed by citing insufficient evidence indefinitely. Adding a minimum observation window (three full fiscal years) sharpens the rule without making it mechanical. The IBM and newspaper cases cited in the rationale both involved deterioration visible across at least five years before action; three years is the minimum defensible threshold.

qualitative_claim: The ability to raise prices without losing meaningful customer volume is the primary operational litmus test of whether a real structural competitive barrier exists
qualitative_rule: Assess whether the business has historically raised prices at or above input cost inflation while maintaining or growing unit volume over a full business cycle; if structural pricing power is absent, any structural competitive barrier claim requires extraordinary additional justification
anti_scope: Does not require prices to rise every year; assesses the structural capacity to raise prices across a cycle without triggering material volume destruction
rationale: The methodology's principal practitioner stated in a 2010 formal inquiry testimony that pricing power is more important than good management. This framework considers it the definitive operational test of whether a structural competitive barrier is real. A leading consumer brand's demonstrated ability to raise prices globally while sustaining volume was central to a 1988 investment thesis.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 confirms no equivalent seed across comparison corpora. Pricing power as the primary operational litmus test for structural competitive barrier existence is a distinctive and falsifiable formulation — it converts a qualitative assertion ('this business has a moat') into an observable historical test ('has it raised prices above input cost inflation while maintaining volume across a cycle'). The anti_scope correctly scopes to structural capacity rather than year-by-year price movement. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: Management that lacks verifiable integrity is disqualifying regardless of business quality or valuation attractiveness
qualitative_rule: If management has a documented history of misleading shareholders, manipulating reported figures, or structuring transactions to benefit insiders at owner expense, reject the investment and do not reconsider on the basis of other merits
anti_scope: Does not apply to operational errors, poor capital allocation decisions, or strategic mistakes; applies specifically and exclusively to integrity failures and demonstrated dishonesty toward owners
rationale: A leading practitioner of this methodology has stated that intelligence and energy without integrity will destroy you. A major retail accounting fraud case and a financial institution misconduct case demonstrate this as a hard veto. Dishonest management converts what appears to be a business asset into a concealed liability that is typically discovered at the worst possible moment.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent management integrity veto in the comparison corpus. The precision of anti_scope — distinguishing integrity failures from operational errors, poor capital allocation, or strategic mistakes — is critical to preventing over-application and is well-calibrated. Hard veto severity is warranted given the evidence base (Tesco, Wells Fargo). No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: Management that retains earnings without creating equivalent or greater market value over time is destroying shareholder capital through deficient capital allocation
qualitative_rule: For every dollar of earnings cumulatively retained over a full business cycle — measured at minimum across ten years or two complete cycles, whichever is shorter — at least one dollar of incremental market capitalization should have been created; a persistent shortfall indicates management should be returning capital to owners rather than retaining it
anti_scope: Does not apply over short measurement windows, at cyclical troughs, or for businesses in legitimate long-duration investment phases; applies to multi-year cumulative patterns only and is a diagnostic flag requiring further investigation, not an automatic rejection trigger
rationale: The retained earnings test is a specific, actionable capital allocation quality filter within this methodology — it directly connects management decision-making to shareholder outcomes. Extending the measurement horizon to ten years (from five) aligns the quantitative data field with the qualitative rule's stated multi-cycle intent and reduces distortion from single-cycle market valuation swings.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent seed. However, the quantitative_rule references `financials.five_year.market_cap_delta_per_cumulative_retained_earnings_dollar` with a five-year horizon, while the qualitative_rule and anti_scope specify 'multi-year cumulative patterns.' These are inconsistent: five years may include a single extended bear market that depresses market cap without reflecting management's actual capital allocation quality. The data field should reference a longer or more flexible horizon — ten years or a full business cycle — to match the anti_scope's stated intent. The threshold of 1.0 is correct and remains unchanged.

qualitative_claim: The investment entity must not use conventional borrowed money that creates exposure to margin calls, fixed maturity dates, or forced liquidation
qualitative_rule: Prohibit structural use of margin accounts, leveraged instruments, or debt-financed investment positions at the portfolio entity level; any capital structure element that can compel selling at unfavorable prices is categorically excluded
anti_scope: Does not prohibit portfolio companies from carrying operational debt appropriate to their business; does not prohibit insurance float from insurance operations; restricts only leverage at the investment entity level that introduces forced-sale risk
rationale: This framework holds that conventional debt is structurally fragile — it introduces margin calls, maturity dates, and forced liquidation risk at precisely the worst times. The insurance float mechanism achieves leverage-like return amplification without this fragility. This explains what otherwise appears contradictory: constant warnings against leverage from a practitioner running one of the most leveraged capital structures in finance.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent seed. The distinction between investment-entity leverage (prohibited) and operating debt or insurance float (permitted) is the methodological crux of what would otherwise appear as a contradiction. The anti_scope handles this precisely. Veto severity at the portfolio entity level is correct — forced liquidation risk is existential and non-recoverable. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: Selling a position requires affirmative, articulable justification; the default state for a high-conviction holding is to hold indefinitely
qualitative_rule: A sell decision must be justified by at least one of: (a) confirmed secular deterioration of the business's structural competitive barrier, (b) discovered management integrity failure, (c) a materially superior capital redeployment opportunity that exceeds the full tax cost of sale, or (d) price at a gross premium to any reasonable intrinsic value estimate; the absence of a strong fresh buy thesis alone does not constitute justification for selling
anti_scope: Does not mean positions can never be sold; establishes that selling is the exception requiring explicit cause, not a default response to price weakness, macro uncertainty, or peer pressure
rationale: The framework's originator stated a holding period of 'forever.' Truly great businesses become more valuable compounding over time. A flagship consumer franchise holding was retained for over three decades including through periods of meaningful overvaluation, with the explanation that the franchise's continuity made patience economically rational.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent sell discipline seed. The four enumerated conditions for permissible sale — secular moat deterioration, management integrity failure, superior redeployment exceeding tax cost, or gross price premium to any reasonable intrinsic value estimate — form a complete, closed set that matches the methodology's observable behavior. The anti_scope (absence of fresh buy thesis is not sell justification) is critical and correctly stated. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: The permanent compounding cost of realizing a capital gain must be explicitly weighed before any sell decision on a position with significant embedded unrealized gains
qualitative_rule: Before finalizing a sell recommendation on a position with material unrealized gains, estimate the after-tax compounding base permanently lost to the tax event and compare it to the expected incremental benefit from redeployment; proceed only if the redeployment benefit materially and durably exceeds this permanent cost
anti_scope: Does not apply to positions with minimal embedded gains or to loss positions; does not restrict tax-loss harvesting; applies specifically to positions where capital gains taxation would represent a material permanent reduction in the long-run compounding base
rationale: A leading practitioner of this approach has held a core long-term position through periods of overvaluation explicitly because the tax cost of selling and rebuying would destroy more long-run value than patience costs. After-tax compounding math pervades this entire methodology and distinguishes it from strategies that optimize on a pre-tax basis.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent after-tax compounding seed. This seed addresses a dimension — permanent tax cost as a compounding base reduction — that is genuinely distinct from seed_08 (when to sell) and captures a quantitative consideration that must be made before finalizing any sell decision on appreciated positions. The anti_scope correctly excludes minimal-gain positions and loss positions. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: Business value must be assessed on distributable economic earnings rather than on reported accounting earnings
qualitative_rule: Define distributable economic earnings as reported net income plus depreciation and amortization, minus the maintenance capital expenditures required to sustain competitive position and unit volume; use this figure as the primary earnings base for all valuation work, not GAAP net income
anti_scope: Does not prohibit using reported earnings as a starting input; requires adjustment for maintenance capex to arrive at the economically meaningful distributable economic earnings figure before any valuation conclusion is drawn
rationale: A prominent value investor defined distributable economic earnings in a published annual letter as the true measure of a business's cash-generation capacity. GAAP earnings can diverge materially from distributable economic earnings in capital-intensive businesses. A canonical asset-light business case is instructive: its model meant virtually all earnings growth translated directly to free cash flow, a distinction invisible in GAAP income.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent distributable economic earnings definition seed. The formula (net income + D&A − maintenance capex) is precise, actionable, and directly cites a primary source. The anti_scope correctly notes that GAAP earnings are a valid starting input, not a prohibited data source. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: Formal multi-year DCF spreadsheet models must not serve as the primary source of valuation conviction
qualitative_rule: Primary valuation must be conducted as a mental exercise: estimate normalized distributable economic earnings, assess a conservative long-run growth rate, compare the implied purchase-price return against the required return threshold; a formal spreadsheet with multi-year projections, explicit terminal value, and WACC is prohibited as the primary valuation method
anti_scope: Does not prohibit quantitative cross-checking or sanity validation; prohibits treating spreadsheet model output as the primary source of conviction rather than as a check on mental reasoning
rationale: The methodology's originator has explicitly stated they do not build multi-year projections with terminal values and WACC. Precision must reside in the quality of thinking, not in the false confidence of spreadsheet arithmetic — a model producing precise wrong answers is worse than a rough correct one.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent valuation method constraint seed. The prohibition on formal multi-year DCF spreadsheet models as the primary conviction source is a distinctive and frequently misunderstood aspect of this methodology — it is not anti-quantitative, it is anti-false-precision. Warning severity (rather than veto) is appropriate because the rule governs process discipline rather than binary investment acceptance. The anti_scope preserving quantitative cross-checking is correct. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: Full qualitative assessment of business quality and competitive durability must be completed and confirmed before examining whether the price is attractive
qualitative_rule: Complete the business quality screens — knowledge boundary, structural competitive barrier existence, structural competitive barrier direction, pricing power, management integrity — before examining price multiples or valuation ratios; a business that fails qualitative screens must not be reconsidered solely because its price appears low
anti_scope: Does not mean price is unimportant or that it is the final consideration; means that price analysis is only valid and meaningful after business quality has been affirmatively confirmed
rationale: Rule 6 of this methodology states explicitly: lead analysis with business quality, not price. The evolution of this methodology from the traditional deep-discount approach was precisely the recognition that a quality-over-cheapness principle outperforms a cheapness-over-quality principle — a cheap price cannot rescue a structurally weak business over a long holding period.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent sequencing rule. Quality-before-price as an explicit process ordering rule is a specific and falsifiable constraint on how analysis must be structured. A business failing qualitative screens cannot be reconsidered solely on price is the critical anti-recency-bias provision. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: A company should pay dividends only when it demonstrably cannot deploy retained capital at returns exceeding what owners could earn independently
qualitative_rule: Evaluate whether a dividend-paying company has genuinely exhausted high-return reinvestment opportunities; if the company is simultaneously paying regular dividends and earning high incremental returns on internally deployed capital, the capital allocation is sub-optimal and signals potential principal-agent misalignment
anti_scope: Does not disqualify dividend-paying companies from the investment universe; means dividend payment should be read as evidence about whether the business has run out of superior capital deployment options, not as a positive signal in itself
rationale: The methodology's capital allocation hierarchy places dividends as the last resort. The reasoning: if management can reinvest at returns exceeding shareholder opportunity cost, retained earnings compound faster inside the business. methodology exemplar firm has never paid a regular dividend because this condition has never been met.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent dividend interpretation seed. Reading dividend payment as a signal about the exhaustion of high-return reinvestment opportunities — rather than as a positive income signal per se — is a distinctive interpretive stance that has direct implications for how the methodology ranks capital allocators. Note severity is appropriate; this is a diagnostic lens, not a screen. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: The purchase price must imply an acceptable return even under a materially conservative, downside-case earnings scenario, not only under the base case
qualitative_rule: Run the valuation with a growth rate materially below the base case and confirm that the implied return at the purchase price remains acceptable relative to the risk-free alternative; if the investment thesis breaks under a plausible downside scenario, the price does not embed sufficient valuation buffer
anti_scope: Does not require discounting all investments to classical deep-discount value levels; does not prohibit paying above-average multiples for exceptional businesses; requires only that the downside-case return remains acceptable before committing capital
rationale: Even within the methodology's evolved framework — where business quality provides a second layer of protection — there is always a price at which even the greatest business becomes a bad investment. In a representative historical case, the investor specifically verified that a downside growth scenario still implied attractive returns at the purchase price before committing.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent downside-scenario valuation requirement. The rule that the purchase price must imply an acceptable return under a materially conservative downside earnings scenario — not only the base case — is a critical margin of safety operationalization that goes beyond simply requiring a discount to fair value. Warning severity and the anti_scope (does not require deep-discount prices for exceptional businesses) are correctly calibrated. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: Management that demonstrably resists the institutional imperative — willing to hold excess cash, decline available acquisitions, and defy peer behavior when economics do not justify action — is a significant positive quality signal
qualitative_rule: Assess whether management has a documented pattern of declining available acquisitions when returns are insufficient, returning excess capital when no superior use exists, and resisting imitative pressure from industry peers; positive evidence includes past instances where management chose deliberate inaction over the pressure to deploy capital
anti_scope: Does not penalize management for making acquisitions per se; assesses specifically whether the pattern shows resistance to empire-building and peer imitation pressure as distinct from genuine opportunity-driven deployment
rationale: This methodology identified the institutional imperative — organizations' tendency to absorb available capital regardless of returns and reflexively imitate peers — as one of the most insidious forces in corporate governance. Managers who resist it are rare and command a meaningful quality premium in this framework's analysis.
_revise_rationale: Phase 2 finds no equivalent institutional imperative resistance seed. Identifying documented management resistance to empire-building and peer imitation pressure as a positive quality signal is a distinctive positive-screen contribution that complements the negative management integrity veto in seed_05. Note severity is appropriate — this is an upside quality indicator, not a pass/fail gate. No modification warranted.

qualitative_claim: Sustained high returns on invested capital over a full business cycle are the primary quantitative signature that a structural competitive barrier is operative
qualitative_rule: Screen for businesses whose average return on invested capital — calculated before goodwill and intangible amortization, over a full decade — materially exceeds a reasonable estimate of the cost of capital; a business consistently earning 15% or more on tangible invested capital in a competitive economy is producing objective evidence that a structural barrier protects its returns; absent this, any claimed structural competitive barrier requires extraordinary additional evidence before acceptance
anti_scope: Does not apply to financial businesses or insurance operations where ROIC as defined is not a structurally comparable metric; does not apply to businesses in early multi-year reinvestment cycles where current ROIC is deliberately suppressed by growth capex; requires decade-long averages to smooth acquisition accounting distortions and cyclical variation; the 15% threshold is a diagnostic trigger for investigation, not a mechanical pass/fail gate
rationale: Sustained high ROIC is the quantitative manifestation of structural competitive advantage — a business compounding capital at materially above-market rates over a decade is providing falsifiable, financial-statement-grounded evidence that competitive forces have not eroded its returns. This seed bridges the qualitative moat existence assessment in seed_02 with a directly observable financial output, creating a cross-validation channel: if the qualitative moat assessment is affirmative but ten-year average ROIC is below 15%, the qualitative assessment requires heightened scrutiny and explicit explanation of the divergence.
